etymology.description: 宀(“roof”) + 至(phonetic, “arrive”/“this exact spot”) = a house or residence under a roof
etymology.interpretation: The character 室(shì, "room") combines the semantic element 宀(shì, "roof") with the phonetic 至(zhì, "arrive"), symbolizing a covered place where one stops or resides. This interpretation aligns with historical uses of 室 to denote a specific location or dwelling.

examples.chinese: 他的书在房间里(他的書在房間裏)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de shū zài fángjiān lǐ.
examples.english: His book is in the room.

examples.chinese: 走进这个房间(走進這個房間)，你会发现它很温馨(你會發現它很溫馨)。
examples.pinyin: Zǒujìn zhègè fángjiān, nǐ huì fāxiàn tā hěn wēnxīn.
examples.english: When you enter this room, you will find it very cozy.

examples.chinese: 会议室在走廊的尽头(會議室在走廊的盡頭)。
examples.pinyin: Huìyì shì zài zǒuláng de jìntóu.
examples.english: The meeting room is at the end of the corridor.
